Grad Cafe Analytics Module 4
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

Flask app

Flask application factory and routes for the GradCafe analytics app.

module_4.src.app.create_app(config: dict | None = None, *, pull_func: Callable[[...], dict] | None = None, query_func: Callable[[...], list[dict]] | None = None) → Flask[source]

Create a testable Flask app with injectable pull and query functions.

Database utilities

Shared PostgreSQL utilities for the GradCafe application.

module_4.src.db_utils.connect(database_url: str | None = None) → Any[source]

Create a psycopg connection using DATABASE_URL-compatible settings.

module_4.src.db_utils.get_database_url(database_url: str | None = None) → str[source]

Return a PostgreSQL connection URL from an argument or environment variables.

Data loading

Create and load the GradCafe PostgreSQL applicants table.

module_4.src.load_data.build_program(record: dict[str, Any]) → str | None[source]

Build the Module 3 program field from cleaned or scraped values.

module_4.src.load_data.build_term(record: dict[str, Any]) → str | None[source]

Build a term string such as Fall 2026 from a scraped record.

module_4.src.load_data.clean_date(date_string: Any) → date | None[source]

Convert GradCafe date strings such as May 29, 2026 to dates.

module_4.src.load_data.clean_float(value: Any) → float | None[source]

Convert GPA and score values to floats when possible.

module_4.src.load_data.clean_gre(value: Any, lowest: float, highest: float) → float | None[source]

Convert GRE values to floats and remove values outside the expected range.

module_4.src.load_data.create_applicants_table(database_url: str | None = None, *, drop_existing: bool = False) → None[source]

Create the applicants table and uniqueness index used by tests and the app.

module_4.src.load_data.insert_applicants(records: Iterable[dict[str, Any]], database_url: str | None = None) → int[source]

Insert records into PostgreSQL without duplicating likely duplicate applicants.

module_4.src.load_data.load_initial_data(database_url: str | None = None, data_file: str = 'C:\\Users\\jctho\\Desktop\\jhu_software_concepts\\module_4\\src\\data\\llm_extend_applicant_data.json') → int[source]

Drop, recreate, and load the applicants table from the Module 3 JSON file.

module_4.src.load_data.load_json_records(data_file: str = 'C:\\Users\\jctho\\Desktop\\jhu_software_concepts\\module_4\\src\\data\\llm_extend_applicant_data.json') → list[dict[str, Any]][source]

Read applicant records from a JSON file.

module_4.src.load_data.next_applicant_id(cur: Any) → int[source]

Return the next integer primary key for applicants.

module_4.src.load_data.normalize_record(record: dict[str, Any], p_id: int) → dict[str, Any] | None[source]

Normalize one JSON/scraped record into the required applicants schema.

Pull data

Pull recent GradCafe data and insert only new rows into PostgreSQL.

module_4.src.pull_data.main() → None[source]

Run a small pull from the command line.

module_4.src.pull_data.pull_and_insert(database_url: str | None = None, *, scraper: ~typing.Callable[[...], list[dict]] = <function scrape_data>, target_records: int = 25) → dict[str, int][source]

Scrape recent GradCafe records and insert new records into the database.

Query data

SQL analysis queries for the GradCafe applicants table.

module_4.src.query_data.format_answer(value: Any, *, percentage: bool = False) → str[source]

Format SQL answers, forcing percentages to use exactly two decimals.

module_4.src.query_data.get_analysis_results(database_url: str | None = None) → list[dict[str, str]][source]

Run the Module 3 analysis queries and return results for the Flask template.

module_4.src.query_data.get_expected_keys() → set[str][source]

Return the keys every analysis result dictionary should contain.

module_4.src.query_data.run_query(cur: Any, question: str, sql: str, *, percentage: bool = False) → dict[str, str][source]

Run one SQL query and return a template-ready result dictionary.

Scraping

” Module 2 GradCafe scraper

This file contains the scraping logic for collecting public

GradCafe graduate admissions applicant data and saving it to applicant_data.json

Current version: -Defines constants -Builds GradCafe URLs with urllib -checks robots.txt with urllib.robotparser

module_4.src.scrape.load_data(path: Path = WindowsPath('data/new_applicant_data.json')) → list[dict[str, str | None]][source]

Load applicant records from a JSON file.

module_4.src.scrape.main() → None[source]

Run a small multi-page scrape test and save the records to applicant_data.json.

module_4.src.scrape.save_data(data: list[dict[str, str | None]], path: Path = WindowsPath('data/new_applicant_data.json')) → None[source]

Save applicant records to a JSON file.

module_4.src.scrape.scrape_data(target_records: int = 25) → list[dict[str, str | None]][source]

Scrape GradCafe applicant records from multiple public survey pages. Resumes from existing applicant_data.json if present.
